Demand Intelligence for Resellers
314,104
Messages
39,888
Mentions
70
Brands
Welcome to your Demand Intelligence Dashboard. This tool analyzes thousands of Discord messages to find what people want to buy, what is trending, and where the biggest sales opportunities are. Every number comes from real conversations in your Discord servers.
314,104
Messages Scanned
39,888
Product Mentions
70
Brands Detected
546
Item Combinations
18
Channels Analyzed
Messages Scanned: Total Discord messages processed. Product Mentions: Messages about a specific brand or item. Brands: Unique brands mentioned. Item Combinations: Unique brand + item pairs (e.g. "Balenciaga hoodie"). Channels: Discord channels with product discussion.

Top 15 Brands by Mentions

Taller bars = more popular. Color shows trend momentum.

What People Are Saying (Intent Breakdown)

Shows the mix of buying signals across your community.
Intent types: Request = looking to buy. Ownership = just bought it. Satisfaction = positive review. Regret = missed opportunity. Neutral = mentioned without clear buying intent.
Top Actions Based on Your Data
  • Stock Various shoes -- 230 people want it but cannot find it
  • Focus on sizes size XL, size 9, size 11 -- most requested
  • Bundle Balenciaga + Travis Scott -- 69 customers want both
Unmet Demand = Sales Opportunity. These items are actively requested but almost nobody has them yet. The bigger the gap between People Asking and People Who Have It, the bigger your opportunity.
Unmet Demand -- Items People Want but Cannot Get
Sorted by demand gap. Higher gap = bigger opportunity.
BrandItemPeople AskingPeople Who Have ItUnmet GapUnique Buyers

Inventory Recommendations
AI-prioritized stocking suggestions based on all demand signals.
Priority levels: HIGH = 10+ unmet requests, stock immediately. MEDIUM = 5-9 unmet, strong opportunity. LOW = 2-4 unmet, worth watching.
PriorityBrandItemDemand GapWhy
HIGHVariousshoes230Very high unmet demand; 138 unique users requesting
HIGHVarioushoodie213Very high unmet demand; 114 unique users requesting
HIGHBalenciagageneral188Very high unmet demand; 100 unique users requesting
HIGHVariouswatch140Very high unmet demand; 91 unique users requesting

HIGHRick Owensgeneral98Very high unmet demand; 91 unique users requesting

Most Requested Sizes

Blue = people asking. Green = people who own it.

Most Requested Colors

Blue = people asking. Green = people who own it.
Size & Color Tip: When the blue bar (requests) is much taller than green (owned), people want it but cannot find it. Prioritize those variants.
Know your customers. We analyze each user's Discord activity to understand buying behavior. Loyal Buyers buy often. High-Intent Prospects ask for a lot but buy little (untapped opportunity). Active Buyers moderate buying. Browsers window shopping. Casual light activity.

Customer Segments

How your community breaks down by buying behavior.

Top Users by Activity

Blue = items requested. Green = items bought.
Customer Profiles
Buy Ratio = what % of requests turned into purchases. 0.50 means half their asks led to buying. Low ratio + high requests = target with offers.
UsernameSegmentRequestsPurchasedHappy ReviewsMissed ItemsBuy Ratio

Cross-Sell Opportunities
Brands the same customers discuss -- bundle these for higher sales.
Cross-sell: If someone likes Brand A, they likely want Brand B too. Use this to create bundles or plan inventory together.
Brand ABrand BCustomers in Common

People who asked about a brand AND later bought it.
High conversion brands are safer to stock heavily -- proven demand.

Search and explore all detected items. Type a brand or item name to filter. Click column headers to sort. Score combines popularity (20%), buying intent strength (50%), and trend momentum (30%) -- higher = more valuable to stock. Velocity shows trend direction: positive = rising, negative = cooling.

Track how demand changes over time. Spikes often correspond to new drops, restocks, or community hype events. The trending table shows what is gaining momentum right now.

Daily Activity by Intent Type

Each color = a different signal. Spikes = high activity days.
Fastest Rising Items
Biggest increase in mentions this period vs. previous period.
Velocity measures momentum. +2.0x means 3x more mentions than last period. High velocity + high requests = about to blow up.
BrandItemThis PeriodLast PeriodVelocity
SupremeUnknown12111.00
Eric EmanuelUnknown716.00

Unknowntee430.33

Unknowncap220.00
Unknownwatch518-0.72

Monthly Activity Patterns

Seasonal trends -- use this to plan inventory for busy months.
Not all channels are equal. WTB (Want to Buy) channels give the strongest buying signals. Pickups show actual purchases. General chat has more noise. Use this to understand where signals come from.

Mentions by Channel

Which channels produce the most product discussion.

Intent Mix per Channel

What type of messages each channel produces.
Signal Strength (color): Green = high-quality buying signals (WTB, pickups). Yellow/red = lower intent (general chat). Prioritize green channels for stocking decisions.
Full Channel Data
ChannelTotalBuy RequestsHappyMissed Opp.PurchasesStrength
general3327510303309511038860.34
wtb34733395190480.67
latest-pickups694152306550.56
quality-control652191906120.42
wts-vouches6468628090.51
wdywt4756204670.55
zr-picks20291212170.42

tier-3-sellers8946020.09
announcements771330140.26
tier-2-sellers7376030.14
tier-1-sellers6203000.03
tier-0-sellers30112020.33
clothes900000.00
curated-by-mav702000.19
poll400000.00
shoes401000.16
custom-order-info110000.65
Four types of demand signals:
Requests -- Actively looking to buy.
Satisfaction -- Positive reviews.
Regret -- Wish they had bought it.
Ownership -- Just received it.

High regret = people want these but missed out. Stock them to capture pent-up demand.
